name: Angel Biscuit
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the buttermilk and stir in the shortening until melted. Allow to cool.
2. Dissolve the yeast in 1/4 cup warm water, allowing it to soften.
3. Add sugar to the yeast mixture.
4. Sift together flour and baking powder into the yeast mixture. Stir until combined.
5. Cover with a damp cloth and an inverted plate. Chill overnight.
6. The dough will keep for about 5 days. On the 6th and 7th day, it can be used as cobbler topping or for fried pies.
7. When ready to use, roll out the dough to 1/4 inch thickness and cut into 1 1/2-inch rounds.
8. Brush each round with melted butter and stack two together.
9. Place in a greased pan, almost touching. Let rise for 1 hour.
10. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes or until golden brown, depending on the size of the biscuits.
11. A good use for Angel Biscuit Dough on the 6th and 7th days is Fried Apple Pies.
